content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms from autonomous agent research can be repurposed to solve the 'stagnant memory drift' problem cutting-edge 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems face, where outdated old memories gradually corrupt new, task-relevant knowledge acquisition. This works by assigning intrinsic curiosity rewards to agents that actively query and refresh underactivated old memories that conflict with new observations, rather than passively decaying or retaining all memories equally.
